notes: Mistral Vibe is the FORK variant (like codex), but its rewind restores BOTH files and conversation. Vibe's Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.vibe/logs/session): a proc-<PID> pre-session appears at launch and hands off to a session_<ts>_<hash>-keyed row (SESSION-1) on the first prompt — birth templated from the codex sibling: session_birth (presession) + pid_bind + v1_session_handoff (new_session). SESSION-1 runs TWO normal turns ('one','two') — the checkpoints. Then /rewind (RewindApp picker, EDIT_AND_RESTORE) truncates the conversation and forks: _reset_session (agent_loop.py:1848) mints a NEW session_id (parent=old) and SessionLogger repoints to a fresh dir, so a NEW session_<...> messages.jsonl (SESSION-2) materializes carrying the rewound history; the original SESSION-1 file lingers frozen (append-only 'a' mode + save_interaction only appends beyond total_messages, so it is NEVER truncated/rewritten → no transcript_removed). The post-rewind turn ('three') runs on SESSION-2 from the rewound point. Three user-observable signals: (1) session stays ready after the rewind (post_rewind_ready dwell + no transcript_removed on the primary); (2) a NEW session_id appears (v2_session_birth new_session:true); (3) the post-rewind turn drives a clean ready→working→ready arc on SESSION-2. agent_supports=yes (EDIT_AND_RESTORE reverts files AND conversation). PROVENANCE CAVEAT (not asserted, not a bug): nothing sets ParentSessionID, so the daemon surfaces the fork as an independent new session_id rather than a parent-linked child — the new row + arc are still observed, only the parent_session_id linkage label is lost. BACKFLOW: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so they are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens and matches kiro-cli (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false).

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of vibe launch
After the first prompt lands, the session_<ts>_<hash>-keyed row appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to SESSION-1 (generous bound to cover first-token latency before vibe writes messages.jsonl)
First user prompt ('one') drives ready → working on SESSION-1
First turn completes back to ready (SESSION-1) — checkpoint 1
Second user prompt ('two') drives ready → working on SESSION-1
Second turn completes back to ready (SESSION-1) — checkpoint 2, the point to rewind back from

Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after /rewind the session stays in ready (the rewind is not a turn) and the original SESSION-1 messages.jsonl lingers frozen (append-only, never truncated → no transcript_removed).

Spec: 'a linked new session_id if the adapter forks' — /rewind forks a fresh session_<...> dir (SESSION-2) never seen before, carrying the rewound history; this is the strongest observable that vibe forked the conversation into a new thread. (Parent linkage label is dropped by the parser — caveat, not asserted.)

Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — the post-rewind prompt ('three') drives SESSION-2 ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on SESSION-1).
Spec: post-rewind turn completes back to ready on SESSION-2 — a clean ready → working → ready arc from the rewound point; final live state is ready.
